codeReview.summary: The feature correctly wires reset to call `deleteSession` before `createSession` in the web path and adds hook coverage, but it introduces a desktop regression: the new Tauri invoke command (`delete_session`) is not implemented or registered in `src-tauri`, so reset will fail in Tauri builds.

issues.description: Tauri mode now calls `invoke('delete_session', { sessionId })`, but there is no matching Tauri command implementation/registration. `src-tauri/src/main.rs` invoke handler list (lines 275-284) includes create/load/compare/export/pair-order/snapshot commands but no `delete_session`; reset will throw an unknown-command error on desktop and cannot complete the intended delete-then-create flow.

New coverage validates hook behavior but does not cover the new `deleteSession` transport helper itself (browser fetch branch and Tauri invoke branch), which allowed the missing desktop command mismatch to slip through.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker procedure does not explicitly require confirming that newly added `services/tauri.ts` invoke commands have corresponding Rust `#[tauri::command]` handlers and registrations unless the feature is framed as a wire-contract change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file `.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md` mandates browser UI checks and frontend validators, but this feature added `invoke('delete_session')` in `frontend/src/services/tauri.ts:73` while `src-tauri/src/main.rs` command registry (lines 275-284 in commit 669e0c8) lacks `delete_session`.

summary: Reviewed feature `m6-wire-delete-session-on-reset` using its features entry, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit `669e0c8` diff. Result is FAIL due to a blocking Tauri runtime regression (missing `delete_session` command).
